tickets.prompt:
Build an example application circuit for **PMA3-83LNW+**.

## Component profile
{
  "part_number": "PMA3-83LNW+",
  "description": "PHEMT-based wideband low-noise MMIC amplifier, 0.4\u20138 GHz, 1.2 dB NF at 2 GHz, +37 dBm OIP3, single 5V/6V supply",
  "manufacturer": "Mini-Circuits",
  "package_types": [
    "12-lead MCLP 3x3x0.89mm (DQ1225)"
  ],
  "supply_voltage_range": "5V or 6V (4.75\u20135.25V or 5.75\u20136.75V)",
  "frequency_range": "0.4 to 8.0 GHz",
  "impedance": "50\u03a9",
  "designator": "U",
  "absolute_max": {
    "dc_voltage": "7V",
    "input_power_continuous_05_to_8GHz": "+16 dBm",
    "input_power_continuous_04_to_05GHz": "+9 dBm",
    "input_power_5min_max": "+19 dBm",
    "total_power_dissipation": "0.95 W",
    "junction_temperature": "150\u00b0C",
    "operating_temp_ground_lead": "-40\u00b0C to +105\u00b0C",

    "p1db_2GHz_6V": "21.7 dBm (typ)",
    "operating_current_6V": "75 mA (typ)",
    "operating_current_5V": "58 mA (typ)"
  },
  "pins": [
    {
      "number": 2,
      "name": "RF-IN",
      "type": "input",
      "description": "RF signal input (pad 2). Connects to RF input and to ground via L1. Optional 100pF blocking capacitor may be added in series before C2 for extra DC isolation.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Connect RF source via C2 (10pF series coupling cap). Add L1 (18nH shunt inductor) from this pad to GND as DC bias return and ESD path. Optional separate 100pF series cap for additional DC isolation \u2014 see datasheet p.3.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 3
    },
    {
      "number": 8,
      "name": "RF-OUT/DC-IN",
      "type": "bidirectional",
      "description": "Combined RF output and DC supply input (pad 8). RF output exits via C3 (100pF DC block). VDD supplied via L2 (39nH RF choke). Both functions share this single pad.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "MANDATORY: connect VDD through L2 (39nH RF choke) to this pad. Connect RF output through C3 (100pF DC block). Both must be present \u2014 missing L2 means no bias; missing C3 puts VDD on RF output and damages downstream components. See Fig.1 p.4.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 3
    },
    {
      "number": 0,
      "name": "GND (Paddle)",
      "type": "gnd",
      "description": "Exposed thermal and electrical ground paddle on bottom of package. Both thermal and electrical ground \u2014 primary current return and heat dissipation path.",
      "active": null,
      "internal_pull": null,
      "requirements": "Must be soldered to PCB ground plane with full solder coverage. Primary thermal path \u2014 poor or partial solder contact causes thermal failure. Use Mini-Circuits suggested PCB layout PL-628. Add ground vias under paddle.",
      "ambiguous": false,
      "datasheet_page": 3
    },
    {
      "number": 1,
      "name": "NC",

      "description": "No internal connection (pad 1). Not used internally. Connected to ground on Mini-Circuits characterization test board.",

    "C3 (100pF output block) is mandatory \u2014 without it VDD appears directly on RF output, damaging downstream components.",
    "Input power must not exceed +16 dBm continuous (0.5\u20138 GHz) or +9 dBm continuous (0.4\u20130.5 GHz). +19 dBm is only permitted for 5 minutes maximum.",
    "ESD Class 1A \u2014 handle with ESD precautions at all times.",
    "C2 is 10pF (not 100pF) \u2014 the optional 100pF in the pad 2 description is a separate series cap that may be added before C2, not a replacement.",
    "Device draws 75mA at 6V or 58mA at 5V \u2014 size power supply trace width accordingly.",
    "Thermal resistance junction-to-ground is 47\u00b0C/W \u2014 verify Tj stays below 150\u00b0C at max operating conditions."
  ],

## All pins (13)
  NUM   NAME                 TYPE         DESCRIPTION
     2  RF-IN                input        RF signal input (pad 2). Connects to RF input and to ground via L1. Optional 100pF blocking capacitor may be added in series before C2 for extra DC isolation.
     8  RF-OUT/DC-IN         bidirectional Combined RF output and DC supply input (pad 8). RF output exits via C3 (100pF DC block). VDD supplied via L2 (39nH RF choke). Both functions share this single pad.
     0  GND (Paddle)         gnd          Exposed thermal and electrical ground paddle on bottom of package. Both thermal and electrical ground — primary current return and heat dissipation path.
     1  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 1). Not used internally. Connected to ground on Mini-Circuits characterization test board.
     3  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 3). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     4  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 4). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     5  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 5). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     6  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 6). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     7  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 7). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     9  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 9). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
    10  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 10). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
    11  NC (float!)          passive      No internal connection (pad 11). NOT connected to ground on test board — explicitly different from all other NC pads.
    12  NC (float!)          passive      No internal connection (pad 12). NOT connected to ground on test board — explicitly different from all other NC pads.

      "name": "Typical application and characterization test circuit (Fig. 1, p.4)",
      "description": "RF path: RF source \u2192 C2 (10pF series) \u2192 pad 2 \u2192 [device] \u2192 pad 8 \u2192 C3 (100pF DC block) \u2192 RF output. Bias path: VDD \u2192 L2 (39nH choke) \u2192 pad 8. C1 (0.01\u00b5F) VDD bypass to GND. L1 (18nH) shunt pad 2 to GND. Pads 1,3\u20137,9\u201310 and Paddle tied to GND. Pads 11,12 left floating.",
      "page": 4
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "Pads 11 and 12 must NOT be connected to ground \u2014 unlike all other NC pads, the datasheet explicitly calls these out as floating on the test board (p.3).",
    "DC supply voltage must not exceed 7V \u2014 permanent damage above this limit.",
    "Exposed paddle MUST be fully soldered to PCB ground plane \u2014 primary thermal path. Partial solder contact causes thermal failure.",
    "L2 (39nH) is mandatory \u2014 it is the only DC bias path to the device. Without it the amplifier will not operate.",
    "C3 (100pF output block) is mandatory \u2014 without it VDD appears directly on RF output, damaging downstream components.",
    "Input power must not exceed +16 dBm continuous (0.5\u20138 GHz) or +9 dBm continuous (0.4\u20130.5 GHz). +19 dBm is only permitted for 5 minutes maximum.",
    "ESD Class 1A \u2014 handle with ESD precautions at all times.",
    "C2 is 10pF (not 100pF) \u2014 the optional 100pF in the pad 2 description is a separate series cap that may be added before C2, not a replacement.",
    "Device draws 75mA at 6V or 58mA at 5V \u2014 size power supply trace width accordingly.",
    "Thermal resistance junction-to-ground is 47\u00b0C/W \u2014 verify Tj stays below 150\u00b0C at max operating conditions."
  ],

## All pins (13)
  NUM   NAME                 TYPE         DESCRIPTION
     2  RF-IN                input        RF signal input (pad 2). Connects to RF input and to ground via L1. Optional 100pF blocking capacitor may be added in series before C2 for extra DC isolation.
     8  RF-OUT/DC-IN         bidirectional Combined RF output and DC supply input (pad 8). RF output exits via C3 (100pF DC block). VDD supplied via L2 (39nH RF choke). Both functions share this single pad.
     0  GND (Paddle)         gnd          Exposed thermal and electrical ground paddle on bottom of package. Both thermal and electrical ground — primary current return and heat dissipation path.
     1  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 1). Not used internally. Connected to ground on Mini-Circuits characterization test board.
     3  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 3). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     4  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 4). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     5  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 5). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     6  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 6). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     7  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 7). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
     9  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 9). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
    10  NC                   passive      No internal connection (pad 10). Not used internally. Connected to ground on characterization test board.
    11  NC (float!)          passive      No internal connection (pad 11). NOT connected to ground on test board — explicitly different from all other NC pads.
    12  NC (float!)          passive      No internal connection (pad 12). NOT connected to ground on test board — explicitly different from all other NC pads.

    "description": "Wideband PLL frequency synthesizer: MAX2870 driven by external TCXO/OCXO reference (10\u2013200MHz at REF_IN). Loop filter (R2A, R2B, C1, C2, C3) connects CP_OUT to TUNE pin for closed-loop operation. Dual differential RF outputs (RFOUTA and RFOUTB) each biased via 27nH RF choke to VCC_RF, with AC-coupled 50\u03a9 output. SPI interface (CLK, DATA, LE) controlled by MCU/FPGA. Three separate LDOs supply VCC_RF, VCC_DIG, and VCC_PLL for best noise isolation.",

      "27nH RF choke from each RF output pin (RFOUTA_P, RFOUTA_N, RFOUTB_P, RFOUTB_N) to VCC_RF",
      "100pF AC-coupling capacitor on RF output to 50\u03a9 load",
      "100nF AC-coupling capacitor at REF_IN",
      "10k\u03a9 pullup on LD (optional, required for analog lock detect mode)"
    ],
    "typical_schematic_notes": "Use star power-supply routing with large decoupling capacitor at central VCC node; branch out to each VCC_ pin with its own bypass cap. Connect all GND_ pins (GND_CP, GND_PLL, GND_RF, GND_VCO, GND_TUNE, GND_DIG, GND_SD) to main board ground plane \u2014 NOT directly to the exposed paddle. Solder EP to ground plane via thermal vias. For fast-lock mode, connect SW to the midpoint of the split shunt resistor in the loop filter. Program all registers twice after power-up with 20ms pause between writes."

## All pins (33)
  NUM   NAME                 TYPE         DESCRIPTION
     1  CLK                  input        Serial Clock Input. Data is latched into the 32-bit shift register on the rising edge of CLK.
     2  DATA                 input        Serial Data Input. Serial data is loaded MSB first; the 3 LSBs identify the register address.
     3  LE                   input        Load Enable Input. When LE goes high, data stored in the shift register is loaded into the appropriate latches.
     4  CE                   input        Chip Enable. A logic-low powers the part down and the charge pump becomes high impedance.
     5  SW                   passive      Fast-Lock Switch. Connect to the loop filter when using fast-lock mode. Leave open/no connect in normal mode.
     6  VCC_CP               power        Power Supply for Charge Pump. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to the pin.
     7  CP_OUT               output       Charge-Pump Output. Connect to external loop filter input.
     8  GND_CP               power        Ground for Charge-Pump. Connect to board ground, not to the exposed paddle.
     9  GND_PLL              power        Ground for PLL. Connect to main board ground plane, not to the exposed paddle.
    10  VCC_PLL              power        Power Supply for PLL. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to the pin.
    11  GND_RF               power        Ground for RF Outputs. Connect to board ground plane, not to the exposed paddle.
    12  RFOUTA_P             output       Open Collector Positive RF Output A. Connect to supply through RF choke or 50Ω load.
    13  RFOUTA_N             output       Open Collector Negative RF Output A. Connect to supply through RF choke or 50Ω load.
    14  RFOUTB_P             output       Open Collector Positive RF Output B. Connect to supply through RF choke or 50Ω load.
    15  RFOUTB_N             output       Open Collector Negative RF Output B. Connect to supply through RF choke or 50Ω load.
    16  VCC_RF               power        Power Supply for RF Output and Dividers. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to the pin.
    17  VCC_VCO              power        VCO Power Supply. Place decoupling capacitors to the analog ground plane.
    18  GND_VCO              power        Ground for VCO. Connect to main board ground plane, not directly to the paddle.
    19  NOISE_FILT           passive      VCO Noise Decoupling. Place a 1µF capacitor to ground.
    20  TUNE                 input        Control Input to the VCO. Connect to output of external loop filter.
    21  GND_TUNE             power        Ground for Control Input to the VCO. Connect to main board ground plane, not directly to the paddle.
    22  RSET                 passive      Charge-Pump Current Range Input. Connect an external resistor to ground to set the minimum CP current. ICP = 1.63/RSET × (1 + CP[3:0]).
    23  BIAS_FILT            passive      VCO Noise Decoupling. Place a 1µF capacitor to ground.
    24  REG                  passive      Reference Voltage Compensation. Place a 1µF capacitor to ground.
    25  LD                   output       Lock Detect Output. Logic-high when PLL is locked, logic-low when unlocked. In analog lock detect mode, this is an open-drain output requiring external pullup.
    26  RFOUT_EN             input        RF Output Enable. A logic-low disables the RF outputs.
    27  GND_DIG              power        Ground for Digital circuitry. Connect to main board ground plane, not directly to the paddle.
    28  VCC_DIG              power        Power Supply for Digital Circuitry. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to pin.
    29  REF_IN               input        Reference Frequency Input. High-impedance input with nominal bias of VCC_DIG/2. AC-couple to reference signal.
    30  MUX_OUT              output       Multiplexed Output and Serial Data Out. Configurable via MUX bits (register 2, bits 28:26) to output lock detect, digital lock, divided reference, R divider output, or serial readback data.
    31  GND_SD               power        Ground for Sigma-Delta Modulator. Connect to main board ground plane, not directly to the paddle.
    32  VCC_SD               power        Power Supply for Sigma-Delta Modulator. Place decoupling capacitors as close as possible to the pin.
    33  EP                   power        Exposed Pad (thermal/ground pad on package bottom). Must be connected to board ground.

It is a\nfollow up device of TC358779XBG, without scalar functionality. The\nHDMI-RX runs at 297 MHz to carry up to 7.2 Gbps video stream.   "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15

It is a\nfollow up device of TC358779XBG, without scalar functionality. The\nHDMI-RX runs at 297 MHz to carry up to 7.2 Gbps video stream. It P-VFBGA80-0707-0.65-001\nWeight: 67 mg (Typ.)\nrequires dual link MIPI DSI Tx, 1 Gbps/data lane, to transmit out a\nmaximum 7.2 Gbps video data.\nThe bridge chip is necessary for current and next generation Application Processors to drive a (dual) DSI\nlink display by using its HDMI Tx output port.\nFeatures\n● HDMI-RX Interface the default configuration.\n HDMI 1.4b - Left or right data can     "typical_schematic_notes": "Decouple every VDD pin individually with 100nF ceramic. VDDA needs additional 1\u00c2\u00b5F bulk cap. BOOT0 to GND for normal boot. NRST filtered with 100nF. Crystal: 8MHz between pins 5/6 with 20pF caps to GND."

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Minimal power supply and reset",
      "description": "3.3 V regulator \u2192 VDD_1/2/3 (pins 24, 36, 48), each with 100 nF to adjacent VSS. VDDA (pin 9) \u2192 1 \u00b5F + 10 nF to VSSA (pin 8). VBAT (pin 1) \u2192 VDD with 100 nF to VSS. NRST (pin 7) \u2192 100 nF to VSS + optional reset button. BOOT0 (pin 44) \u2192 10 k\u03a9 to VSS. PB2 (pin 20) \u2192 10 k\u03a9 to VSS. 1 \u00b5F bulk cap on 3.3 V rail.",
      "page": 28
    },
    {
      "name": "HSE 8 MHz crystal oscillator",
      "description": "8 MHz crystal between OSC_IN (pin 5) and OSC_OUT (pin 6). Each pin \u2192 load cap (~20 pF) \u2192 VSS. Configure RCC to HSE + PLL\u00d79 = 72 MHz SYSCLK.",
      "page": 28
    },
    {
      "name": "USB Full-Speed device interface",
      "description": "PA11 (pin 32, USBDM) \u2192 22 \u03a9 \u2192 USB D-. PA12 (pin 33, USBDP) \u2192 22 \u03a9 \u2192 USB D+. D+ \u2192 1.5 k\u03a9 to 3.3 V. GND \u2192 VSS. REQUIRES HSE crystal for accurate 48 MHz USB clock.",
      "page": 31
    },
    {
      "name": "SWD debug header",
      "description": "PA13/SWDIO (pin 34) \u2192 100 \u03a9 \u2192 header SWDIO. PA14/SWCLK (pin 37) \u2192 100 \u03a9 \u2192 header SWCLK. GND, 3.3 V, optional NRST. Compatible with ST-Link/J-Link/CMSIS-DAP.",
      "page": 31
    },
    {
      "name": "CAN via TJA1050 (PB8/PB9 remap)",
      "description": "Enable CAN remap in AFIO_MAPR. PB9/CANTX (pin 46) \u2192 TJA1050 TXD. PB8/CANRX (pin 45) \u2192 TJA1050 RXD. 120 \u03a9 termination at each CAN bus end. Use PB8/PB9 when USB is also active on PA11/PA12.",
      "page": 32
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "BOOT0 (pin 44) MUST be pulled low with 10 k\u03a9 to VSS \u2014 never leave floating. Floating BOOT0 causes random boot mode at power-up.",
    "PB2/BOOT1 (pin 20) should also be pulled low with 10 k\u03a9 to VSS. Floating BOOT1 is undefined.",
    "VDDA (pin 9) must be decoupled with 1 \u00b5F + 10 nF to VSSA (pin 8), not just to the main ground. VDD min is 2.4 V (not 2.0 V) when ADC is in use.",
    "PC13/PC14/PC15 (pins 2\u20134) are powered via internal switch: max 2 MHz, ~3 mA, 30 pF. Cannot drive LEDs or current loads.",
    "VBAT (pin 1) must NOT be left floating. Tie to VDD and add 100 nF if no battery backup needed.",
    "JTAG pins (PA13/PA14/PA15/PB3/PB4) are occupied after reset. Disable JTAG via AFIO_MAPR to use as GPIO/SPI. PB4/NJTRST (pin 40) needs 10 k\u03a9 pull-up to VDD if JTAG not used.",
    "USB and CAN share PA11/PA12 on the default mapping \u2014 cannot be used simultaneously. Remap CAN to PB8/PB9 via AFIO_MAPR when USB is required.",
    "USB requires an HSE crystal for the PLL to generate an accurate 48 MHz USB clock. The internal 8 MHz HSI RC is not accurate enough.",
    "USB D+ (PA12) needs a 1.5 k\u03a9 pull-up to 3.3 V for full-speed enumeration. STM32F103CB has an internal pull-up but it must be enabled in firmware.",
    "All three VDD/VSS pin pairs (24/23, 36/35, 48/47) must ALL be connected and individually decoupled \u2014 connecting only one VDD pin causes malfunction at 72 MHz."
  ],
  "notes": "48-pin LQFP, 7x7 mm body, 0.5 mm pitch, 1.60 mm max height. STM32F103C8 = identical pinout with 64 KB Flash. 37 GPIOs when JTAG disabled; 32 with JTAG enabled. PA0-PA7 and PB5 are NOT 5V tolerant; all other GPIO are FT (5V tolerant). GPIO output speeds: 2/10/50 MHz per pin. USB and CAN cannot share PA11/PA12 simultaneously \u2014 remap CAN to PB8/PB9. HSE crystal mandatory for USB. KiCad footprint: Package_QFP:LQFP-48_7x7mm_P0.5mm."
}

## All pins (48)
  NUM   NAME                 TYPE         DESCRIPTION
     1  VBAT                 power        Backup domain supply voltage (1.8V to 3.6V). Powers RTC, backup registers, and LSE oscillator when main VDD is off.
     2  PC13                 bidirectional Port C bit 13. Alternate functions: TAMPER-RTC (tamper event input or RTC output). Low-speed I/O: max 2MHz, 3mA output current.
     3  PC14                 bidirectional Port C bit 14. Alternate function: OSC32_IN (32.768 kHz crystal input for RTC). When used as GPIO, limited to 2MHz/3mA.
     4  PC15                 bidirectional Port C bit 15. Alternate function: OSC32_OUT (32.768 kHz crystal output for RTC). When used as GPIO, limited to 2MHz/3mA.
     5  OSC_IN               input        High-speed external (HSE) oscillator input, 4â€“16 MHz. Can be remapped to PD0 GPIO after HSE bypass. Drives the PLL for 72 MHz CPU clock.
     6  OSC_OUT              output       High-speed external (HSE) oscillator output. Connects to crystal. Can be remapped to PD1 GPIO after HSE enable.
     7  NRST                 input        Active-low system reset input/output. Internally filtered (>300ns). Also driven low by internal reset sources (POR, WWDG, IWDG).
     8  VSSA                 power        Analog ground reference for ADC. Must be at the same potential as VSS.
     9  VDDA                 power        Analog supply voltage for ADC, reset, and oscillators. Must be â‰¥ VDD.
    10  PA0                  bidirectional Port A bit 0. Alternate: WKUP (standby wakeup), ADC12_IN0, USART2_CTS, TIM2_CH1_ETR.
    11  PA1                  bidirectional Port A bit 1. Alternate: ADC12_IN1, USART2_RTS, TIM2_CH2.
    12  PA2                  bidirectional Port A bit 2. Alternate: ADC12_IN2, USART2_TX, TIM2_CH3.
    13  PA3                  bidirectional Port A bit 3. Alternate: ADC12_IN3, USART2_RX, TIM2_CH4.
    14  PA4                  bidirectional Port A bit 4. Alternate: ADC12_IN4, SPI1_NSS, USART2_CK, TIM3_CH2 (remap).
    15  PA5                  bidirectional Port A bit 5. Alternate: ADC12_IN5, SPI1_SCK.
    16  PA6                  bidirectional Port A bit 6. Alternate: ADC12_IN6, SPI1_MISO, TIM3_CH1, TIM1_BKIN (remap).
    17  PA7                  bidirectional Port A bit 7. Alternate: ADC12_IN7, SPI1_MOSI, TIM3_CH2, TIM1_CH1N (remap).
    18  PB0                  bidirectional Port B bit 0. Alternate: ADC12_IN8, TIM3_CH3, TIM1_CH2N (remap).
    19  PB1                  bidirectional Port B bit 1. Alternate: ADC12_IN9, TIM3_CH4, TIM1_CH3N (remap).
    20  PB2                  bidirectional Port B bit 2. Also functions as BOOT1: sampled at reset together with BOOT0 to select boot mode. High = boot from SRAM; Low (with BOOT0 high) = boot from System Memory.
    21  PB10                 bidirectional Port B bit 10. Alternate: I2C2_SCL, USART3_TX, TIM2_CH3 (remap).
    22  PB11                 bidirectional Port B bit 11. Alternate: I2C2_SDA, USART3_RX, TIM2_CH4 (remap).
    23  VSS_1                power        Digital ground (VSS). One of three VSS pins on LQFP48.
    24  VDD_1                power        Digital supply voltage 2.0Vâ€“3.6V. One of three VDD pins on LQFP48.
    25  PB12                 bidirectional Port B bit 12. Alternate: SPI2_NSS, I2C2_SMBAI, USART3_CK, TIM1_BKIN.
    26  PB13                 bidirectional Port B bit 13. Alternate: SPI2_SCK, USART3_CTS, TIM1_CH1N.
    27  PB14                 bidirectional Port B bit 14. Alternate: SPI2_MISO, USART3_RTS, TIM1_CH2N.
    28  PB15                 bidirectional Port B bit 15. Alternate: SPI2_MOSI, TIM1_CH3N.
    29  PA8                  bidirectional Port A bit 8. Alternate: USART1_CK, TIM1_CH1, MCO (master clock output â€” can output SYSCLK/HSI/HSE/PLL/2).
    30  PA9                  bidirectional Port A bit 9. Alternate: USART1_TX, TIM1_CH2.
    31  PA10                 bidirectional Port A bit 10. Alternate: USART1_RX, TIM1_CH3.
    32  PA11                 bidirectional Port A bit 11. Alternate: USART1_CTS, CAN_RX, USBDM (USB Dâˆ’ differential line), TIM1_CH4.
    33  PA12                 bidirectional Port A bit 12. Alternate: USART1_RTS, CAN_TX, USBDP (USB D+ differential line), TIM1_ETR.
    34  PA13                 bidirectional Port A bit 13. Default after reset: JTMS/SWDIO (SWD debug data I/O). GPIO only available when JTAG/SWD remapped.
    35  VSS_2                power        Digital ground (VSS). One of three VSS pins on LQFP48.
    36  VDD_2                power        Digital supply voltage 2.0Vâ€“3.6V. One of three VDD pins on LQFP48.
    37  PA14                 bidirectional Port A bit 14. Default after reset: JTCK/SWCLK (SWD clock). GPIO only when JTAG/SWD remapped.
    38  PA15                 bidirectional Port A bit 15. Default after reset: JTDI (JTAG data input). Remap available: TIM2_CH1_ETR, SPI1_NSS.
    39  PB3                  bidirectional Port B bit 3. Default after reset: JTDO/TRACESWO (JTAG data output / SWO trace). Remap: TIM2_CH2, SPI1_SCK, SPI3_SCK.
    40  PB4                  bidirectional Port B bit 4. Default after reset: NJTRST (JTAG reset). Remap: TIM3_CH1, SPI1_MISO.
    41  PB5                  bidirectional Port B bit 5. Alternate: I2C1_SMBAI, SPI1_MOSI, TIM3_CH2.
    42  PB6                  bidirectional Port B bit 6. Alternate: I2C1_SCL, USART1_TX (remap), TIM4_CH1.
    43  PB7                  bidirectional Port B bit 7. Alternate: I2C1_SDA, USART1_RX (remap), TIM4_CH2.
    44  BOOT0                input        Boot mode selection bit 0. Sampled at reset: BOOT0=0 â†’ boot from Flash (normal); BOOT0=1 + BOOT1(PB2)=0 â†’ boot from System Memory (USB DFU/UART bootloader); BOOT0=1 + BOOT1=1 â†’ boot from SRAM.
    45  PB8                  bidirectional Port B bit 8. Alternate: TIM4_CH3, I2C1_SCL (remap), CAN_RX (remap).
    46  PB9                  bidirectional Port B bit 9. Alternate: TIM4_CH4, I2C1_SDA (remap), CAN_TX (remap).
    47  VSS_3                power        Digital ground (VSS). One of three VSS pins on LQFP48.
    48  VDD_3                power        Digital supply voltage 2.0Vâ€“3.6V. One of three VDD pins on LQFP48.

Produce a compact, routable PCB layout that matches the example circuit above.
Place components sensibly (decoupling caps close to power pins, etc.) and
pre-route as many traces as possible.

The layout JSON must include:
- `components[]`: each with `id`, `ref`, `x`, `y`, `rotation`, `layer` (F|B), `pads[]`
- `traces[]`: each with `net`, `layer` (F.Cu|B.Cu), `width`, `segments[]`
  where each segment has `start{x,y}`, `end{x,y}`
- `nets[]`: each with `name`, `pads[]` (list of "REF.padnum" strings)
- `board`: {width, height, units:"mm"}
- `vias[]` (optional): each with `x`,`y`,`net`,`drill`,`size`

    "VDDA (pin 9) must be decoupled with 1 \u00b5F + 10 nF to VSSA (pin 8), not just to the main ground. VDD min is 2.4 V (not 2.0 V) when ADC is in use.",

    "USB and CAN share PA11/PA12 on the default mapping \u2014 cannot be used simultaneously. Remap CAN to PB8/PB9 via AFIO_MAPR when USB is required.",

    "USB D+ (PA12) needs a 1.5 k\u03a9 pull-up to 3.3 V for full-speed enumeration. STM32F103CB has an internal pull-up but it must be enabled in firmware.",

## All pins (48)
  NUM   NAME                 TYPE         DESCRIPTION
     1  VBAT                 power        Backup domain supply voltage (1.8V to 3.6V). Powers RTC, backup registers, and LSE oscillator when main VDD is off.
     2  PC13                 bidirectional Port C bit 13. Alternate functions: TAMPER-RTC (tamper event input or RTC output). Low-speed I/O: max 2MHz, 3mA output current.
     3  PC14                 bidirectional Port C bit 14. Alternate function: OSC32_IN (32.768 kHz crystal input for RTC). When used as GPIO, limited to 2MHz/3mA.
     4  PC15                 bidirectional Port C bit 15. Alternate function: OSC32_OUT (32.768 kHz crystal output for RTC). When used as GPIO, limited to 2MHz/3mA.
     5  OSC_IN               input        High-speed external (HSE) oscillator input, 4â€“16 MHz. Can be remapped to PD0 GPIO after HSE bypass. Drives the PLL for 72 MHz CPU clock.
     6  OSC_OUT              output       High-speed external (HSE) oscillator output. Connects to crystal. Can be remapped to PD1 GPIO after HSE enable.
     7  NRST                 input        Active-low system reset input/output. Internally filtered (>300ns). Also driven low by internal reset sources (POR, WWDG, IWDG).
     8  VSSA                 power        Analog ground reference for ADC. Must be at the same potential as VSS.
     9  VDDA                 power        Analog supply voltage for ADC, reset, and oscillators. Must be â‰¥ VDD.

    40  PB4                  bidirectional Port B bit 4. Default after reset: NJTRST (JTAG reset). Remap: TIM3_CH1, SPI1_MISO.
    41  PB5                  bidirectional Port B bit 5. Alternate: I2C1_SMBAI, SPI1_MOSI, TIM3_CH2.
    42  PB6                  bidirectional Port B bit 6. Alternate: I2C1_SCL, USART1_TX (remap), TIM4_CH1.
    43  PB7                  bidirectional Port B bit 7. Alternate: I2C1_SDA, USART1_RX (remap), TIM4_CH2.
    44  BOOT0                input        Boot mode selection bit 0. Sampled at reset: BOOT0=0 â†’ boot from Flash (normal); BOOT0=1 + BOOT1(PB2)=0 â†’ boot from System Memory (USB DFU/UART bootloader); BOOT0=1 + BOOT1=1 â†’ boot from SRAM.
    45  PB8                  bidirectional Port B bit 8. Alternate: TIM4_CH3, I2C1_SCL (remap), CAN_RX (remap).
    46  PB9                  bidirectional Port B bit 9. Alternate: TIM4_CH4, I2C1_SDA (remap), CAN_TX (remap).
    47  VSS_3                power        Digital ground (VSS). One of three VSS pins on LQFP48.
    48  VDD_3                power        Digital supply voltage 2.0Vâ€“3.6V. One of three VDD pins on LQFP48.

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

- `package_types[]`: list of package strings (e.g. ["DIP-8", "SOIC-8"])

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

Required fields:
- `part_number`: exact part number string
- `description`: one-line description
- `symbol_type`: one of ic|npn|pnp|nmos|pmos|resistor|capacitor|inductor|diode|led|opamp|amplifier
- `package_types[]`: list of package strings (e.g. ["DIP-8", "SOIC-8"])
- `pins[]`: every pin — `number`, `name`, `type` (input|output|power|gnd|io|passive|nc), `description`
- `supply_voltage_range`: e.g. "3.0V – 5.5V"
- `max_current_ma`: number
- `required_passives[]`: {type, value, placement} for caps/resistors always needed
- `datasheet_url`: if known

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

    A1  REXT                 passive      External reference resistor terminal for HDMI-RX automatic termination resistance adjustment. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.

    A3  DDC_SCL              bidirectional DDC I2C slave clock. Schmitt trigger, 5V tolerant, open drain. Powered by VDDIO33.

    J6  A_SD2                output       I2S audio data signal bit 2 output. Powered by VDDIO18.

    J8  A_SD1                output       I2S audio data signal bit 1 output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    J9  A_SD0                output       I2S audio data bit 0 / TDM data output. Powered by VDDIO18.

    K1  PCKIN                input        Audio PLL Reference Input clock. PLL input. Powered by VDDIO33.
    K2  PFIL                 output       Audio PLL Low Pass Filter signal for phase comparator. Powered by VDDIO33.
    K3  I2C_SDA              bidirectional I2C slave data. Open drain. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K4  I2C_SCL              bidirectional I2C slave clock. Open drain. Supports 100 kHz / 400 kHz / 2 MHz. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K5  A_WFS                output       I2S word clock / TDM frame sync output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K6  VDDC11               power        1.1V internal core power supply (ball K6).
    K7  A_SCK                output       I2S / TDM bit clock signal output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K8  RESETN               input        System reset input, active low. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K9  REFCLK               input        Reference clock input, 40–50 MHz. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.

Produce a compact, routable PCB layout that matches the example circuit above.
Place components sensibly (decoupling caps close to power pins, etc.) and
pre-route as many traces as possible.

The layout JSON must include:
- `components[]`: each with `id`, `ref`, `x`, `y`, `rotation`, `layer` (F|B), `pads[]`
- `traces[]`: each with `net`, `layer` (F.Cu|B.Cu), `width`, `segments[]`
  where each segment has `start{x,y}`, `end{x,y}`
- `nets[]`: each with `name`, `pads[]` (list of "REF.padnum" strings)
- `board`: {width, height, units:"mm"}
- `vias[]` (optional): each with `x`,`y`,`net`,`drill`,`size`

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

    "layout_notes": [
      "BGA PACKAGE: P-VFBGA80-0707-0.65-001. Body 7x7mm, 0.65mm pitch, 80 balls. Requires 4-layer PCB minimum with controlled impedance stackup.",
      "BGA PAD STYLE: Use NSMD (Non Solder Mask Defined) pads, 0.35mm diameter circles. Solder mask opening 0.45mm. Do NOT use SMD (solder mask defined) pads -- Cu defined for fine-pitch BGA.",
      "VIA FANOUT: Dogbone vias, 0.25mm drill / 0.5mm pad. Single escape trace 0.1mm width between ball pads. Via fence spacing must be >0.15mm from BGA pads. For power balls use shorter traces (<0.3mm) before via.",
      "LAYER STACKUP (4-layer): F.Cu (components + fanout) / In1.Cu (GND plane, solid copper pour) / In2.Cu (power planes: +3V3, +1V2, +1V8 pours) / B.Cu (back-side decoupling caps + signal routing). Target impedance: 50 Ohm single-ended, 100 Ohm differential.",
      "DECOUPLING CAPS (C9-C15): All placed on B.Cu (back side of PCB) directly below their respective power balls. They connect through the BGA fanout vias on F.Cu via the power plane on In2.Cu. Place within 1mm of associated via.",
      "POWER SEQUENCING: DVDD33 must come up FIRST before VDD12 and VDDIO18. Use power-enable sequencing via STM32 GPIOs or an RC delay. VDD12 and VDDIO18 can come up simultaneously.",
      "MIPI DSI ROUTING (F1-G7 area, right side): Route CDSI0 lanes as 100 Ohm differential pairs. Match lengths within pair to +-5mil. Match all 5 pairs (D0-D3 + CLK) to within +-50mil. DSI1 balls (H1-J7) leave as NC (no-connects) for single-link operation.",
      "HDMI TMDS ROUTING (A2-B7 area, left side): Route as 100 Ohm differential pairs toward HDMI connector. Match TMDS pair lengths within +-5mil. Match all 4 pairs to within +-100mil. Shield pins (A4, A7, B2, B5) connect directly to GND plane via.",
      "REXT RESISTOR (R11, 4.7k): Place within 2mm of C4 ball. Short trace from C4 via to R11, then short stub to GND via. This is a mandatory bias calibration resistor -- do not omit.",
      "I2C LINES (D1=SCL, D2=SDA): Route through B.Cu via vias. Pull-ups R3/R4 (4.7k to +1V8) placed near TC358870. Trace length to STM32 is non-critical (I2C is slow), max 10cm.",
      "INT/BOOT-STRAP (D3): At power-on, this pin is sampled to set I2C address. Pulled high (via R5 10k to +1V8) = address 0x1F. For 0x0F, add 0R to GND (R6 -- populate to force low at boot). After boot, INT becomes open-drain interrupt output -- R5 pull-up remains active.",
      "RESET_N (D4): Connect to STM32 PA8 via 0.15mm trace. Add 100nF cap to GND within 5mm (not shown separately -- part of STM32 area). Drive low for >=100us to reset TC358870.",
      "HDMI 5V DETECT (D5): HDMI connector pin 18 (+5V from source) connects via R8/R9 voltage divider (47k/10k) to this ball. TC358870 VDDIO18=1.8V, so max input at D5 must be <=1.8V. 5V * 10k/(47k+10k) = 0.88V -- within spec.",
      "HPD (C3): Hot Plug Detect output. Drive high when TC358870 is ready. Connect to HDMI connector pin 19 via R7 (1k series) for source current limiting. Pull-up on HDMI source side is ~100k to 5V.",
      "DDC LINES (C1=DDC_SCL, C2=DDC_SDA): Connect directly to HDMI connector DDC pins. No pull-up needed here -- source provides pull-ups. Optionally add 4.7k to +3.3V for robustness.",
      "BOARD SIZE TARGET: <43mm x 27mm. Suggested placement: TC358870 in center-right area, STM32 center-left, LDOs at left, HDMI connector at bottom-left edge, USB-C at top-left, FPC at right edge.",
      "BGA INSPECTION: Use X-ray or AXI after reflow to verify BGA solder joint quality. Thermal profile critical for 0.65mm pitch. Use standard Sn-Ag-Cu (SAC305) solder paste."
    ],

    A3  DDC_SCL              bidirectional DDC I2C slave clock. Schmitt trigger, 5V tolerant, open drain. Powered by VDDIO33.

    J3  INT                  bidirectional Dual-function: during RESETN asserted, input — detects pull polarity to select I2C slave address (pull-up → 0x1F, pull-down → 0x0F). After RESETN deasserted, becomes interrupt output (active high). Normal digital. Powered by VDDIO18.
    J4  A_OSCK               output       Audio Over Sampling Clock output (256fs). Powered by VDDIO18.
    J5  A_SD3                output       I2S audio data signal bit 3 output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    J6  A_SD2                output       I2S audio data signal bit 2 output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    J7  VDDIO18              power        1.8V IO supply for system, audio, IR, I2C interfaces. Can also operate at 3.3V.
    J8  A_SD1                output       I2S audio data signal bit 1 output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    J9  A_SD0                output       I2S audio data bit 0 / TDM data output. Powered by VDDIO18.
   J10  VDD12_MIPI0          power        MIPI DSI 1.2V supply for link 0 (DSI0 / CDSI TX0).
    K1  PCKIN                input        Audio PLL Reference Input clock. PLL input. Powered by VDDIO33.
    K2  PFIL                 output       Audio PLL Low Pass Filter signal for phase comparator. Powered by VDDIO33.
    K3  I2C_SDA              bidirectional I2C slave data. Open drain. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K4  I2C_SCL              bidirectional I2C slave clock. Open drain. Supports 100 kHz / 400 kHz / 2 MHz. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K5  A_WFS                output       I2S word clock / TDM frame sync output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K6  VDDC11               power        1.1V internal core power supply (ball K6).
    K7  A_SCK                output       I2S / TDM bit clock signal output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K8  RESETN               input        System reset input, active low. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K9  REFCLK               input        Reference clock input, 40–50 MHz. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.
   K10  VSS                  gnd          Ground (ball K10).

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

      },

        "id": "l_int",

        "y": 300

      {
        "id": "l_test",
        "text": "R3: TEST pin ALWAYS pull low (mandatory)",
        "x": 40,
        "y": 380
      },
      {
        "id": "l_ddc_scl",
        "text": "R4: DDC_SCL 1k to +5V | R6: 100k to GND",
        "x": 40,
        "y": 460
      },
      {
        "id": "l_ddc_sda",
        "text": "R5: DDC_SDA 4.7k to +5V | R7: 100k to GND",
        "x": 40,
        "y": 540
      },
      {
        "id": "l_hpdi",
        "text": "R8: HPDI 100k to +5V (HDMI pin 18)",
        "x": 40,
        "y": 780
      },

        "id": "l_resetn",

        "x": 940,
        "y": 600

        "y": 640

        "text": "CRITICAL: VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 \u2014 use reverse-current protection switch or regulator",

  "layout_example": {
    "version": "1.0",
    "title": "TC358870XBG VFBGA-80 Layout Example with Full Decoupling Network",
    "board": {
      "width": 30,
      "height": 22,
      "units": "mm"
    },
    "component": {
      "ref": "U1",
      "value": "TC358870XBG",
      "footprint": "VFBGA-80 (P-VFBGA80-0707-0.65-001)",
      "package": "80-ball VFBGA, 7x7mm body, 0.65mm pitch, 9 rows (A-J) x 7 cols",
      "x": 15,
      "y": 11,
      "rotation": 0
    },
    "recommended_clearances": {
      "bga_pad_to_pad": 0.25,
      "bga_escape_trace": 0.1,
      "bga_escape_space": 0.1,
      "diff_pair_width": 0.1,
      "diff_pair_gap": 0.12,
      "diff_pair_clearance": 0.15,
      "power_trace": 0.25,
      "via_drill": 0.25,
      "via_pad": 0.5,
      "pad_to_mask": 0.05,
      "min_annular_ring": 0.125,
      "bga_breakout_channel": 0.1
    },
    "layout_notes": [
      "BGA PACKAGE: P-VFBGA80-0707-0.65-001. Body 7x7mm, 0.65mm pitch, 80 balls. Requires 4-layer PCB minimum with controlled impedance stackup.",
      "BGA PAD STYLE: Use NSMD (Non Solder Mask Defined) pads, 0.35mm diameter circles. Solder mask opening 0.45mm. Do NOT use SMD (solder mask defined) pads -- Cu defined for fine-pitch BGA.",
      "VIA FANOUT: Dogbone vias, 0.25mm drill / 0.5mm pad. Single escape trace 0.1mm width between ball pads. Via fence spacing must be >0.15mm from BGA pads. For power balls use shorter traces (<0.3mm) before via.",
      "LAYER STACKUP (4-layer): F.Cu (components + fanout) / In1.Cu (GND plane, solid copper pour) / In2.Cu (power planes: +3V3, +1V2, +1V8 pours) / B.Cu (back-side decoupling caps + signal routing). Target impedance: 50 Ohm single-ended, 100 Ohm differential.",
      "DECOUPLING CAPS (C9-C15): All placed on B.Cu (back side of PCB) directly below their respective power balls. They connect through the BGA fanout vias on F.Cu via the power plane on In2.Cu. Place within 1mm of associated via.",
      "POWER SEQUENCING: DVDD33 must come up FIRST before VDD12 and VDDIO18. Use power-enable sequencing via STM32 GPIOs or an RC delay. VDD12 and VDDIO18 can come up simultaneously.",
      "MIPI DSI ROUTING (F1-G7 area, right side): Route CDSI0 lanes as 100 Ohm differential pairs. Match lengths within pair to +-5mil. Match all 5 pairs (D0-D3 + CLK) to within +-50mil. DSI1 balls (H1-J7) leave as NC (no-connects) for single-link operation.",
      "HDMI TMDS ROUTING (A2-B7 area, left side): Route as 100 Ohm differential pairs toward HDMI connector. Match TMDS pair lengths within +-5mil. Match all 4 pairs to within +-100mil. Shield pins (A4, A7, B2, B5) connect directly to GND plane via.",
      "REXT RESISTOR (R11, 4.7k): Place within 2mm of C4 ball. Short trace from C4 via to R11, then short stub to GND via. This is a mandatory bias calibration resistor -- do not omit.",
      "I2C LINES (D1=SCL, D2=SDA): Route through B.Cu via vias. Pull-ups R3/R4 (4.7k to +1V8) placed near TC358870. Trace length to STM32 is non-critical (I2C is slow), max 10cm.",
      "INT/BOOT-STRAP (D3): At power-on, this pin is sampled to set I2C address. Pulled high (via R5 10k to +1V8) = address 0x1F. For 0x0F, add 0R to GND (R6 -- populate to force low at boot). After boot, INT becomes open-drain interrupt output -- R5 pull-up remains active.",
      "RESET_N (D4): Connect to STM32 PA8 via 0.15mm trace. Add 100nF cap to GND within 5mm (not shown separately -- part of STM32 area). Drive low for >=100us to reset TC358870.",
      "HDMI 5V DETECT (D5): HDMI connector pin 18 (+5V from source) connects via R8/R9 voltage divider (47k/10k) to this ball. TC358870 VDDIO18=1.8V, so max input at D5 must be <=1.8V. 5V * 10k/(47k+10k) = 0.88V -- within spec.",
      "HPD (C3): Hot Plug Detect output. Drive high when TC358870 is ready. Connect to HDMI connector pin 19 via R7 (1k series) for source current limiting. Pull-up on HDMI source side is ~100k to 5V.",
      "DDC LINES (C1=DDC_SCL, C2=DDC_SDA): Connect directly to HDMI connector DDC pins. No pull-up needed here -- source provides pull-ups. Optionally add 4.7k to +3.3V for robustness.",
      "BOARD SIZE TARGET: <43mm x 27mm. Suggested placement: TC358870 in center-right area, STM32 center-left, LDOs at left, HDMI connector at bottom-left edge, USB-C at top-left, FPC at right edge.",
      "BGA INSPECTION: Use X-ray or AXI after reflow to verify BGA solder joint quality. Thermal profile critical for 0.65mm pitch. Use standard Sn-Ag-Cu (SAC305) solder paste."
    ],

    A3  DDC_SCL              bidirectional DDC I2C slave clock. Schmitt trigger, 5V tolerant, open drain. Powered by VDDIO33.

    J8  A_SD1                output       I2S audio data signal bit 1 output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    J9  A_SD0                output       I2S audio data bit 0 / TDM data output. Powered by VDDIO18.

    K3  I2C_SDA              bidirectional I2C slave data. Open drain. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K4  I2C_SCL              bidirectional I2C slave clock. Open drain. Supports 100 kHz / 400 kHz / 2 MHz. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K5  A_WFS                output       I2S word clock / TDM frame sync output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K6  VDDC11               power        1.1V internal core power supply (ball K6).
    K7  A_SCK                output       I2S / TDM bit clock signal output. Powered by VDDIO18.
    K8  RESETN               input        System reset input, active low. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.

  "application_circuits": [
    {
      "name": "Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)",
      "description": "HDMI source \u2192 HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) \u2192 internal TMDS Rx PHY \u2192 YUV/RGB FiFo \u2192 dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine \u2192 CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine \u2192 CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) \u2192 DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector \u2192 DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host \u2192 I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.",
      "page": 7
    },
    {
      "name": "DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)",
      "description": "HDMI connector DDC_SCL \u2192 1k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA \u2192 4.7k\u03a9 pull-up to +5V and 100k\u03a9 pull-down to GND \u2192 DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) \u2192 100k\u03a9 to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.",
      "page": 15
    },
    {
      "name": "Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)",
      "description": "VDD33_HDMI \u2192 1.5k\u03a9 bias \u2192 BIASDA (J1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) \u2192 5pF to GND \u2192 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base \u2192 collector via 1.0k\u03a9 \u2192 PCKIN (K1) [0.1\u03bcF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0k\u03a9 to GND. PFIL (K2) \u2192 0.1\u03bcF + 1000pF to GND.",
      "page": 16
    },
    {
      "name": "Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)",
      "description": "5V \u2192 3.3V reg \u2192 external switch on TMDS signal lines \u2192 VDD33_HDMI (>10\u03bcF\u00d73 + >1\u03bcF + >0.1\u03bcF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDC11; 3.3V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO33; 1.8V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDDIO18; 1.2V reg \u2192 FB \u2192 VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.",
      "page": 17
    }
  ],
  "common_mistakes": [
    "TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally \u2014 never leave floating or pull high",
    "IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used \u2014 do not leave floating",
    "INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10k\u03a9 pull resistor serves both functions.",
    "VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).",
    "BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1\u03bcF when Audio PLL is not used",
    "REXT (A1) MUST have 2k\u03a9\u00b11% to VDD33_HDMI \u2014 mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration",
    "DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent",
    "All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI",
    "Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode",
    "No-ball positions (C3\u2013C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3\u2013H8) are physically absent \u2014 do not attempt connections at these grid positions"
  ],
  "notes": "80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7\u00d77mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half \u2014 both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40\u201350 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.",

  "category": "bridge",
  "layout_example": {
    "version": "1.0",
    "title": "TC358870XBG VFBGA-80 Layout Example with Full Decoupling Network",
    "board": {
      "width": 30,
      "height": 22,
      "units": "mm"
    },
    "component": {
      "ref": "U1",
      "value": "TC358870XBG",
      "footprint": "VFBGA-80 (P-VFBGA80-0707-0.65-001)",
      "package": "80-ball VFBGA, 7x7mm body, 0.65mm pitch, 9 rows (A-J) x 7 cols",
      "x": 15,
      "y": 11,
      "rotation": 0
    },
    "recommended_clearances": {
      "bga_pad_to_pad": 0.25,
      "bga_escape_trace": 0.1,
      "bga_escape_space": 0.1,
      "diff_pair_width": 0.1,
      "diff_pair_gap": 0.12,
      "diff_pair_clearance": 0.15,
      "power_trace": 0.25,
      "via_drill": 0.25,
      "via_pad": 0.5,
      "pad_to_mask": 0.05,
      "min_annular_ring": 0.125,
      "bga_breakout_channel": 0.1
    },
    "layout_notes": [
      "BGA PACKAGE: P-VFBGA80-0707-0.65-001. Body 7x7mm, 0.65mm pitch, 80 balls. Requires 4-layer PCB minimum with controlled impedance stackup.",
      "BGA PAD STYLE: Use NSMD (Non Solder Mask Defined) pads, 0.35mm diameter circles. Solder mask opening 0.45mm. Do NOT use SMD (solder mask defined) pads -- Cu defined for fine-pitch BGA.",
      "VIA FANOUT: Dogbone vias, 0.25mm drill / 0.5mm pad. Single escape trace 0.1mm width between ball pads. Via fence spacing must be >0.15mm from BGA pads. For power balls use shorter traces (<0.3mm) before via.",
      "LAYER STACKUP (4-layer): F.Cu (components + fanout) / In1.Cu (GND plane, solid copper pour) / In2.Cu (power planes: +3V3, +1V2, +1V8 pours) / B.Cu (back-side decoupling caps + signal routing). Target impedance: 50 Ohm single-ended, 100 Ohm differential.",
      "DECOUPLING CAPS (C9-C15): All placed on B.Cu (back side of PCB) directly below their respective power balls. They connect through the BGA fanout vias on F.Cu via the power plane on In2.Cu. Place within 1mm of associated via.",
      "POWER SEQUENCING: DVDD33 must come up FIRST before VDD12 and VDDIO18. Use power-enable sequencing via STM32 GPIOs or an RC delay. VDD12 and VDDIO18 can come up simultaneously.",
      "MIPI DSI ROUTING (F1-G7 area, right side): Route CDSI0 lanes as 100 Ohm differential pairs. Match lengths within pair to +-5mil. Match all 5 pairs (D0-D3 + CLK) to within +-50mil. DSI1 balls (H1-J7) leave as NC (no-connects) for single-link operation.",
      "HDMI TMDS ROUTING (A2-B7 area, left side): Route as 100 Ohm differential pairs toward HDMI connector. Match TMDS pair lengths within +-5mil. Match all 4 pairs to within +-100mil. Shield pins (A4, A7, B2, B5) connect directly to GND plane via.",
      "REXT RESISTOR (R11, 4.7k): Place within 2mm of C4 ball. Short trace from C4 via to R11, then short stub to GND via. This is a mandatory bias calibration resistor -- do not omit.",
      "I2C LINES (D1=SCL, D2=SDA): Route through B.Cu via vias. Pull-ups R3/R4 (4.7k to +1V8) placed near TC358870. Trace length to STM32 is non-critical (I2C is slow), max 10cm.",
      "INT/BOOT-STRAP (D3): At power-on, this pin is sampled to set I2C address. Pulled high (via R5 10k to +1V8) = address 0x1F. For 0x0F, add 0R to GND (R6 -- populate to force low at boot). After boot, INT becomes open-drain interrupt output -- R5 pull-up remains active.",
      "RESET_N (D4): Connect to STM32 PA8 via 0.15mm trace. Add 100nF cap to GND within 5mm (not shown separately -- part of STM32 area). Drive low for >=100us to reset TC358870.",
      "HDMI 5V DETECT (D5): HDMI connector pin 18 (+5V from source) connects via R8/R9 voltage divider (47k/10k) to this ball. TC358870 VDDIO18=1.8V, so max input at D5 must be <=1.8V. 5V * 10k/(47k+10k) = 0.88V -- within spec.",
      "HPD (C3): Hot Plug Detect output. Drive high when TC358870 is ready. Connect to HDMI connector pin 19 via R7 (1k series) for source current limiting. Pull-up on HDMI source side is ~100k to 5V.",
      "DDC LINES (C1=DDC_SCL, C2=DDC_SDA): Connect directly to HDMI connector DDC pins. No pull-up needed here -- source provides pull-ups. Optionally add 4.7k to +3.3V for robustness.",
      "BOARD SIZE TARGET: <43mm x 27mm. Suggested placement: TC358870 in center-right area, STM32 center-left, LDOs at left, HDMI connector at bottom-left edge, USB-C at top-left, FPC at right edge.",
      "BGA INSPECTION: Use X-ray or AXI after reflow to verify BGA solder joint quality. Thermal profile critical for 0.65mm pitch. Use standard Sn-Ag-Cu (SAC305) solder paste."
    ],
